---
title: "ECLIPS Internal Workshop 2"
author: "Frank DiTraglia & Ludovica Gazze"
institute: "Preliminary Results"
format: 
  beamer:
    aspectratio: 169
    theme: default
    header-includes: |
      \setbeamertemplate{footline}{\hfill\insertframenumber\,/\,\inserttotalframenumber\hspace{2em}\vspace{1ex}} 
      \usepackage{tikz}
      \usetikzlibrary{positioning}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows}
      \usetikzlibrary{shapes}
      \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta}
---

## Phase I: Recruiting / Study Participation Decision 

**Clean up this flowchart: turn it into a Frank/Ludovica birds-eye view of Phase I and Phase II. Then "zoom in" on specific parts and fill in details. Could be more like Angela's flowchart initial boxes. E.g. addresses, letters, randomization, re-balancing, dust kits,feedback**

\centering
  \begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.5]
  \node (households) [draw,align=center] {All Leeds Residential Addresses \\ $\approx 373,000$};
  \node (letters) [draw, below of=households, node distance=2cm,align=center] {Send 200,000 Letters};  
  \node (ineligible) [draw, dashed, below left of=letters, align=center, node distance = 2.5cm] {$\approx180,000$ \\ Ineligible};  
  \node (eligible) [draw, below right of=letters, align=center, node distance = 2.5cm] {$\approx 20,000$ \\ Eligible};  
  \node (respond) [draw, below right of=eligible, align=center, node distance=2.5cm] {$\approx 2000$ \\ Baseline Surveys};  
  \node (nonrespond) [draw, dashed, below left of=eligible, align=center, node distance=2.5cm] {No Response \\ $\approx 18,000$};  

  \draw[->] (households) -- (letters) node[midway, right]{Randomly Sample Addresses};
  \draw[->, dashed] (letters) -- (ineligible);
  \draw[->] (letters) -- (eligible) node[midway, right]{$10\%$ with Young Children};
  \draw[->] (eligible) -- (respond) node[midway, right]{$10\%$ Response };
  \draw[->, dashed] (eligible) -- (nonrespond);
\end{tikzpicture}

5 GBP gift card for sign-up and baseline survey completion


## Research Question 1: who responds and why?

- Demographic characteristics (don't observe at the individual level but do observe at LSOA)
- Design of recruiting materials

# Design

## Sampling Design

- What is our sampling frame? What kinds of exclusion criteria? (removed student residences, hotels, commerical blah blah)
- What external data sources did we use? (Census, EPCs, Leeds Council Tax, Postal Codes)
- How / why did we stratify and what did we stratify on? Estimate share eligible (child 1 to 6; how we approximated it)
- Also stratify by housing age and LSOA (say how many LSOAs)

## Map of where we sent letters

- Map of where we sent letters for both rounds; possibly next to or alongside a plot of the estimated share eligible across LSOAs.

## Describe the treatments
- This slide gives the text and the motivation
- One treatment is only for old houses
- Deifnition of old house

## Show the different recruiting letters
- This slide shows images (or put on prev slide if it fits)

## Re-balancing / Kit Allocation
- Recruiting phase is to find people who will get kits
- Intentionally recruit more people than kits so we can re-balance
- Why re-balance? We expect that response related to demographic characteristics and lead exposure is too but in opposite directions
- How we re-balanced and how we assigned kits: another RCT here with two kit types.
- Could just say what the totals were for each round without explaining the results in detail. 
- (For the moment don't talk about the two round aspect; that comes next)

## Map of where we sent kits?

- Haven't looked at this yet; might look quite different because of re-balancing

## Adaptive Design

- Everything above is correct but doesn't explain how we broke Phase I (Intake) into two rounds
- 150k total letters of which 30k in round 1 and adaptive round 2
- Explain how / why we did this adaptively and our pre-registered rule
- Explain how the re-balancing interacted with the two-stage process

# Results

## Description of outcomes
- Define "response" as response within 45 days of sending letter
- Define "usable" response as: GP, valid other stuff... (check this)
- (Is there anything else? Maybe; if so add here)

# Basic Results
- How many usable responses did we get? Overall / (stable by round) / by treatment / by age of house
- This is not a set of regressions (per se) it's just raw shares by cell (treatment / kind of house / histogram or map by LSOA)
- Could compare against our protocol where we predicted response rates based on similar studies; could talk about the per participant cost of recruiting in this way (need the total letter cost)

## More results: heterogeneity etc

- Frank did a bunch of crazy stuff! 
- Various regressions; can we show them graphically? (Probably yes: use a map)
- Can we show regressions? (Yes we can!)
- Index of multiple deprivation is particularly interesting here but it's actually *not* a census characteristic. Should point out where we got it from.

## Non-usable results
- Are there any treatment effects for *clicking on the survey* or *starting the survey* even if you don't finish?
- Does this vary with anything we can observe? Treatment / housing age etc?
- This will require Frank to check with Caner; something is weird about the updated anonymized data: doesn't seem to have removed any of the "testing" rows

## Survey 

- Perhaps start with some basic descriptives from the survey
- Would be nice to say something like: based on census data (and where we sent letters) we would expect: the overall sample / this MSOA to have such and such shares of different kinds of people 
- In the sample we actually had: give characteristics. 
- Intake survey: did people know about lead already? Are we getting people who already knew all about lead or people who didn't know much about lead in advance?


## Phase II: Collecting Blood Samples 

Baseline survey & census: choose probs of sending each household a kit (2000 to 500)

\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.4]
   \node (kits) [draw, align = center]{Send 500 Blood Test Kits \\ and Midline Surveys};
   \node (pack) [draw, below right of=kits, align = center, node distance = 2.5cm]{Control\\ 250 Kits};
   \node(nopack)[draw, below left of =kits, align = center, node distance = 2.5cm]{Treatment \\ 250 Kits};
   \draw[->] (kits) -- (pack) node[midway, right]{Randomize Kits};
   \draw[->] (kits) -- (nopack);
   \node (blood) [draw, below left of=pack,align = center, node distance = 2.5cm]{$\approx 70$ Returned Kits \\ \& Midline Surveys};
   \node (nobloodnopack) [draw, dashed, below left of=nopack, node distance = 2.5cm, align = center]{No \\ Response};
   \node (nobloodpack) [draw, dashed, below right of=pack, node distance = 2.5cm, align = center]{No \\ Response};
   \draw[->] (nopack) -- (blood); 
   \draw[->] (pack) -- (blood) node[midway, right]{14\% Response};
   \node (experience) [draw, below of=blood, align = center, node distance = 1.5cm]{Send 200 Experience Surveys};
   \draw[->, dashed] (nopack) -- (nobloodnopack); 
   \draw[->, dashed] (pack) -- (nobloodpack);
   \draw[->, dashed] (nobloodnopack) -- (experience);
   \draw[->, dashed] (nobloodpack) -- (experience);
   \draw[->] (blood) -- (experience);
\end{tikzpicture}

35 GBP for returned sample / midline survey; 10 GBP for experience survey


## Who returns a blood sample?
- Needs to be put into the repo in the anonymized form and merged.
- Talk to Andrea about this today! 
- Consider adding some very preliminary results about who sends back a kit (valid kit?)
- Could look at this by kit type; could correlate with demographics / knowledge about lead etc. These need to be re-weighted so they're slightly trickier. Use the re-balancing weights.

## Who returns the exposure survey?

- Ask Andrea about this; she has sent the vouchers for this so we can probably modify her existing code to do this; should already be anonymized and ready-to-use? 
- Can correlate this with the same things as returning a blood sample: kit type, demographics, knowledge about lead, etc.

## Feedback survey
 
- It was opened a few days ago we think? Ask Andrea
- Perhaps we could present some very preliminary results?
